notes: W10D1 — first push of the form-first cycle. Big spotlight wins on push press: parallel feet locked in, 40s genuinely hard with proper form, slight bar drift forward (less than KB presses, no T-shape), chest pushed out / rib flare on set 1 — caught it and controlled for it on subsequent sets. Slight crackle showing up on the right shoulder by the third set but 40 is the right working weight. Lateral raise breakthrough: leading with elbow shot a line of pain from scapula down to elbow (not super painful) on set 1 — rolled shoulders, engaged/disengaged scapula between sets, sets 2 and 3 felt way better, set 3 actually hard 'in the right way'. Right pec is super sore today, definitely connected to shoulder — chest pressing slow magnifies the link. Form-over-load reality check: 40lb DBs on incline press at 30° actually felt harder than expected, died at 7-9 reps, didn't need 2-up-2-down. 30° incline + automatic forearm stack is the move; going wider to T feels awkward and lower than I used to do it. Cable lat raise crackle improving with right-side pec massage between sets — last 3 sets hard but more muscle than crackle, just nerve tip at elbow. Cues feedback: cable lat raise 'run line behind body' didn't make sense, ignored it. Incline overhead tri done kneeling on cable column, not following written notes. Several descriptions are off from actual movement — flagged for cue cleanup.

notes: W10D2 — first KB session back after 3 weeks (W8 Tue/Thu skipped, W9 sick). Re-acquaintance worked: home-base 25lb on the skill block felt right, and the spotlights both produced wins. Banded prep swap: used the blue rope band held in hands instead of the mini-band — shoulders and scaps felt 'like a massage' afterward, very relaxed going into the bent press / windmill work. B-stance flow at 25lb x 3 each side x 3 rounds felt great — almost too light, briefly worried about overdoing it, but a few hours later confirmed it was the right dose. Cossacks were the surprise: very hard to do slow and deep, but once the groove kicked in they felt really good; hit a stop at the end (range/depth limit, not failure). TGU spotlight breakthrough: bumped UP to 40lb after 25 felt grooved — the focus on the clean itself made each side feel weightless, including 40. Left side still harder than right (especially the to-standing transition), but left is more *correct* because it's weaker and gets the deliberate reps; right is stronger but sloppier. C&P spotlight landed exactly on plan: 35lb good, 40lb good — projection: start at 40 next week, work toward 45. Net: had a workout AND a good stretch, left feeling strong. The 'Tuesday is skill/mobility flank' framing held — fresher, not cooked. No QL talk on swings.

sets.weight: blue rope band, held (swapped from mini-band)

Round 1 at prescribed 25. Left harder than right but better than the first time back. Going to standing is the hardest checkpoint on the left.

Bumped UP to 40 (vs prescribed 25) after R1 felt grooved. **Focus on the clean itself made each side feel weightless** — even at 40. Right side stronger; left side more *correct* because it's weaker and gets deliberate reps. The weak-side-first protocol is producing better quality on the left.
Round 1 — good. On plan.
Round 2 — good. **Could probably start at 40 next week and work toward 45.** The 35→40→45→50 ramp is on track for next 2-3 KB sessions. No QL talk.
Cooldown row, light. Per plan.

notes: W10D3 — first pull of the form-first cycle. Pull-up spotlight win: discovered the rack's adjustable climber-style handles — narrow / normal / wide across sets, 6 slow (not to chin, focus on engaging back) / 6 / 6 / 4+2. The handles felt really natural (W11D3 clarified they don't actually rotate — adjustable, not a true climber hold). Reality check: gained ~15lb recently, pull-ups are getting harder — quality reps the priority, not chasing W7's 32. Incline DB curl swap (preacher taken): 25lb good — too many reps on set 1 (12), dialed to 10s with slow stable form; left side hard at 7, used right arm to lightly assist last 3 reps. DB reverse fly at 25 (heavier than prescribed 22.5 — had the weights ready, slow leading-with-elbows felt great). DB row pyramid + chest-supported row both went well — slow and controlled, all sets doable, no need to push up. Face pulls 42.5 (machine doesn't do 45) — set 1 nailed slow, set 2 hard at 17, rested more set 3 hard at 19. Lat pulldown 140 (no 5s on the machine, so not 145) — three sets solid, rested more before the last set, full 12 reps slow. **Equipment-specificity gap (recurring):** the workout descriptions don't say WHICH machine to use, and the session logs haven't been recording it either, so weights from prior weeks aren't comparable. Today: high cable curl on single pulley (100 easy → 120 harder/slow), cable crunch on single-pulley regular machine (57.5x15 easy). The setup rotates week to week based on what's free — fix is to make the workout descriptions setup-agnostic AND require the log to capture which machine was used. Bigger meta-feedback: workouts should be reorganized as STATION focuses to minimize machine-hopping — currently bouncing between cable column / Kinesis / single pulley / DBs / racks within the same day.

notes: W10D5 — first legs of the form-first cycle. Order jumped around due to machine availability (squat → leg press → calf raise → seated leg curl → incline RDL → lunges). Squat spotlight at 65/side x 8 x 4: felt 'pretty good and solid' going slow, except for a 'weightless' / energy-gap sensation in the middle of the rep — bottom solid, top solid, middle feels like the legs aren't really working. **This is biomechanically normal**: in the mid-range of a back squat, the moment arms shift such that hip and knee leverage are both partially favorable, so it's the easiest zone of the lift (think strength curve, not uniform load). Feeling lighter through the middle = you're moving smoothly through the easy zone. The bottom and top are where the real work happens — and that's where the brace + drive matter. Last 2 sets got better as the brace pattern locked in. Still got super sweaty — confirms 65 is enough work even when slow. Leg press easy, 'hard not to add more' — but as soon as the depth went a little deeper the QL started engaging, so backed off. Confirms the 3-plate medium-ROM hold week is doing exactly what it should: protecting the QL, not chasing load. Calf raise: really hard slow — dying at 15, needs other-foot support for the negatives. Flag: **might need both feet for 3-4s tempo** when going slow — single-leg + slow eccentric together is too much. Seated leg curl breakthrough: **dropped to 50 and moved the pad around the ankles** (per the prescribed cue 'ankles UNDER the pad') and it felt more correct — clearly was doing it wrong before with the pad too high on the calf. The W8/W10 prescribed 100 was working through a wrong lever; 50 with correct ankle placement is genuinely working the hamstrings. **Start W11 at 50 with correct ankle placement and rebuild from there** — don't carry forward the wrong-form 100 number. Incline RDL: first set easy, second set way harder. Left side legs really tight (the W7/W8 hammie tightness pattern is back). Slow tempo is hard — confirms the 'slow IS the stimulus' lesson. Lunges 25lb light by feel but 'very very hard' — didn't feel like dying though, **no QL spasms**. Big confidence boost: the QL spasm origin lift handled the easing-back load. Net: 'really good overall, interesting how going much lighter still felt like a workout' — the form-first cycle is working as designed. **(Naming corrected W11D5: this lift was prescribed as 'back extension' through W10; it's the incline RDL on the 45° bench. Exercise ID corrected here.)**
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back
